narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A fleet that sits at a healthy baseline most of the time but is catastrophically under-provisioned during two predictable daily spike windows on weekdays. The fleet is fixed-capacity; scheduled scaling would absorb the spikes without affecting baseline cost much.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests time-pattern recognition. The Compute Analyst must see beyond the baseline averages to the predictable spike windows, and recommend a scaling policy that targets those windows specifically — not a blanket capacity increase.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Implement predictive auto-scaling with scheduled capacity increases during 10–11 AM and 3–4 PM weekday windows. Expected outcome: p95 spikes drop below 65% while maintaining SLA and reducing baseline cost by 48%.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation says "add auto-scaling." A great recommendation identifies the specific spike windows and proposes scheduled scaling rather than reactive scaling (which would lag the spikes).

before_after_evidence.config_after: Predictive scheduled scaling 10–11 AM and 3–4 PM, baseline m5.large × 6
before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: p95 spikes dropped below 65%, SLA 99.9% maintained, baseline cost reduced 48% due to right-sized off-peak capacity.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across checkout-service cohort during Q4 2025.
